g: --- CASE 1: Van Zandt County BESS Moratorium — Belltown Power / Taaleri Energia / Amador Energy Storage Project, Van Zandt County, TX ---

Action Taken: Announced Amador Energy Storage Project (100MW/200MWh BESS) approximately 11 miles southwest of Canton, Van Zandt County, TX
Legal Mechanism: N/A — project announcement

Action Taken: Presented Amador BESS project to Van Zandt County Commissioners Court at workshop meeting
Legal Mechanism: Commissioner's Court presentation

Entity: Ronald Lane, Nancy White, and 18 other Van Zandt County residents
Action Taken: Filed lawsuit (Cause No. 24-00204) seeking damages, temporary and permanent injunction against BT Amador Storage LLC, Maria Amador, Jose Valedelmar Amador, RES Group LLC, Belltown Power Texas Land 2 LLC, and CSC Trust Company of Delaware
Legal Mechanism: Petition for Damages, Temporary and Permanent Injunction and Jury Demand — 294th Judicial District Court, Van Zandt County

Entity: Van Zandt County residents (100+ in attendance)
Action Taken: Public meeting held where residents voiced safety concerns including risk of fire and environmental damage from lithium battery explosion
Legal Mechanism: Public meeting

Entity: Van Zandt County Fire Marshal (Kevin Palmer)
Action Taken: Issued order to Taaleri Energia to conform to all applicable NFPA standards per Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233 and NFPA 855 (2020 ed). Required: permit application, UL container test results, site survey, hazard mitigation plan, emergency response plan, permanent water source documentation. Compliance deadline: April 7, 2025
Legal Mechanism: Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233; NFPA 855 (2020 ed)

Entity: Van Zandt County District Attorney (Tonda Curry) / 294th District Court Judge Chris Martin
Action Taken: DA presented ex parte petition for Temporary Restraining Order. Judge Martin signed TRO (Cause No. 25-00067) against Taaleri Energia North America LLC, BT Amador Storage LLC, Amador BESS Holdings Inc., Renewable Energy Systems Americas Inc., and Belltown Texas Power 2 LLC. TRO in place for 14 days, prohibiting batteries from entering the county until NFPA compliance achieved
Legal Mechanism: Temporary Restraining Order — 294th Judicial District Court, Van Zandt County; Texas Health & Safety Code

Action Taken: Court hearing scheduled for July 24, 2025 to determine whether TRO will be extended or batteries allowed in
Legal Mechanism: Temporary Restraining Order extension proceeding

Entity: Van Zandt County officials (Fire Marshal Kevin Palmer, DA Tonda Curry, Commissioner Cliff Williams)
Action Taken: Confirmed TRO still in effect. No compliant safety test results received from developer. Groundwork continues but no batteries permitted in county
Legal Mechanism: Ongoing TRO enforcement

Action Taken: Unanimously adopted temporary moratorium freezing all proposed green energy developments (solar farms, wind installations, BESS) in unincorporated areas. Passed after hours of public testimony
Legal Mechanism: Texas Health & Safety Code §121.003 (county police powers for health, safety, and welfare)

g: --- ADDITIONAL ESCALATING CASES (TX BESS / SOLAR+STORAGE ≥50 MW WITH DISQUALIFIER-LEVEL OPPOSITION, 2023–2026) ---
--- CASE 8: Flat Rock Energy Center — East Point Energy / Kendall County (Comfort), TX (BESS) ---

Action Taken: withdrawn — East Point Energy formally cancelled the 250 MW Flat Rock Energy Center BESS on 2026-01-27 after sustained county-level opposition, the adoption of restrictive Kendall County BESS standards (2024 IFC + protective designs), and Kendall County's joint 391 Sub-Regional Planning Commission with Kerr County to oppose BESS development. State Rep. Ellen Troxclair publicly hailed the cancellation as a victory.
Legal Mechanism: Restrictive county ordinance + sustained community opposition

Action Taken: Equinor subsidiary East Point Energy began developing the 250 MW Flat Rock Energy Center on 10 acres at Flat Rock Creek Road, Comfort, Kendall County, TX (adjacent to LCRA substation)
Legal Mechanism: Project initiation

Entity: Comfort residents (~275+ attendees) / Kendall County officials
Action Taken: More than 275 residents crowded into Comfort Middle School to voice opposition to East Point's Flat Rock Energy Center and Key Capture Energy's adjacent Ringtrail Ridge BESS (3-acre site on 8-acre plat); raised concerns about thermal runaway lithium fires, toxic byproducts, contaminated runoff, and limited rural firefighting capacity
Legal Mechanism: Public meeting / community opposition

Action Taken: Began strengthening Kendall County safety standards for BESS facilities; consulted with nationally-recognized fire/life-safety expert; implemented protective design requirements
Legal Mechanism: County BESS safety ordinance

Action Taken: Adopted 2024 International Fire Code and implemented restrictive design measures applicable to BESS facilities; effectively raised compliance burden for proposed projects
Legal Mechanism: International Fire Code 2024 adoption (Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233)

Entity: Kendall County Commissioners Court / Kerr County Commissioners Court
Action Taken: Kendall County Commissioners Court voted to move toward establishment of a Sub-Regional Planning Commission ("391 Commission") with Kerr County to strengthen joint opposition to BESS development under Texas Local Government Code Chapter 391; approved resolution requesting TCEQ deny all permits for BESS facilities proposed in the county
Legal Mechanism: Texas Local Government Code Chapter 391 (Sub-Regional Planning Commission)

Entity: State Rep. Ellen Troxclair / Texas Legislature
Action Taken: Filed multiple Texas state bills (HB 5569, HB 5572, HB 1378, SB 819) to give counties authority to regulate BESS facilities, require fire safety standards, and impose setback rules — explicitly motivated by Kendall/Comfort situation
Legal Mechanism: Texas Legislature 89R session BESS bills

Entity: East Point Energy (Catherine Chapman, Manager of Community Engagement)
Action Taken: Issued letter formally cancelling the Flat Rock Energy Center: "Due to shifting strategic priorities, East Point Energy's development of the Flat Rock Energy Center will no longer be pursued at this time"
Legal Mechanism: Project cancellation announcement

Action Taken: Publicly declared victory: "East Point is officially calling off their plans for a battery storage facility" — framed cancellation as a major victory after fierce local opposition
Legal Mechanism: Press statement / political declaration

Action Taken: delayed_in_litigation / ongoing_dispute — 100.8 MW BESS proposed for Reservation Rd at IH-10 (Mountain Home substation), Precinct 4, Kerr County. Kerr County Commissioners Court rejected developer's tax abatement request 2024-07-26; Court adopted two formal resolutions opposing BESS 2024-08-26 and forwarded to TCEQ and state legislators; Kerr County formed Hill Country Energy 391 Sub-Regional Planning Commission with Kendall County to oppose BESS. Project was "expected to be fully operational by 2027" per developer but is materially impeded.
Legal Mechanism: Multiple disqualifiers: opposing county resolutions + 391 commission opposition + tax abatement denial

Entity: Black Mountain Energy Storage II, LLC / ERCOT
Action Taken: ERCOT generation interconnection request entered queue (ERCOT-25INR0210) for 100.8 MW BESS at "7750 LCRA MOUNTAIN HOME SUB" in Kerr County (queue date 02-01-2025)
Legal Mechanism: ERCOT Generation Interconnection Process

Entity: Kerr County Commissioners Court (Commissioners Letz, Paces, et al.)
Action Taken: Rejected Black Mountain Energy Storage's request for property tax abatement on $87 million BESS project at Reservation Rd / IH-10 adjacent to Mountain Home substation; eight speakers spoke against the project at the meeting; Commissioner Letz: "I don't see your application going anywhere"; Commissioner Paces told developer "I guess you've figured out that you are not welcome in Kerr County"
Legal Mechanism: Tax abatement denial; Texas Tax Code Chapter 312

Action Taken: Signed two formal resolutions opposing BESS in Kerr County: one to be sent to the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ, the BESS-permitting agency); one to be sent to State Rep-elect Wes Virdell and State Senator Pete Flores
Legal Mechanism: County resolution opposing BESS (Texas Local Government Code Chapter 81)

Action Taken: Sent letter to Black Mountain Energy Storage CEO Rhett Bennett pressing for answers regarding the proposed Mountain Home BESS facility
Legal Mechanism: Congressional inquiry letter

Action Taken: Held workshop on forming a Chapter 391 Sub-Regional Planning Commission with Kendall County to formally oppose BESS development across both counties; commissioners unanimously agreed to share the proposed interlocal agreement with Kendall County
Legal Mechanism: Texas Local Government Code Chapter 391 (Sub-Regional Planning Commission)

Action Taken: Approved hire of contracted (part-time) fire marshal and adopted a new legally vetted resolution requiring BESS developers to prove compliance with state safety and fire codes before receiving court approval; rescinded earlier May 2025 resolution as a weaker measure
Legal Mechanism: Resolution requiring full BESS compliance documentation; Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233

Entity: Kerr County Commissioner Rich Paces (President, Hill Country Energy SRPC)
Action Taken: Told Lower Colorado River Authority Board of Directors that the 391 SRPC had submitted four coordination notices asking LCRA to consult on proposed BESS sites and that LCRA had refused to meet; called LCRA's refusal a failure of "moral obligation to coordinate planning for BESS with our subregional planning commission"
Legal Mechanism: Texas Local Government Code Chapter 391; LCRA Act

g: --- CASE 7: Apex Clean Energy / SA Grid Solutions Great Kiskadee Storage — Hidalgo County, TX ---

Entity: Apex Clean Energy / SA Grid Solutions (JV with SK Gas / SK eternix)
Action Taken: Outcome: BUILT — 100 MW / 200 MWh BESS in Hidalgo County reached commercial operations on 2025-02-18. No resident lawsuit, no opposing county resolution, no SUP/zoning denial, no fire marshal enforcement order, no court injunction/TRO, no county-level moratorium identified.
Submitted ERCOT generation interconnection request (queue ID ERCOT-23INR0166) for 103.05 MW battery storage project in Hidalgo County, TX
Legal Mechanism: ERCOT Generation Interconnection Process (Nodal Operating Guide §6)

Entity: Apex Clean Energy / SK Gas / SK eternix
Action Taken: Established SA Grid Solutions joint venture; Great Kiskadee Storage assigned as first project owned under JV
Legal Mechanism: Joint venture formation

Entity: First Citizens Bank Energy Finance / Apex Clean Energy / SA Grid Solutions
Action Taken: Closed >$150M financing for Great Kiskadee Storage and a second Texas BESS project; First Citizens served as sole bookrunner and co-lead arranger; project under construction in Hidalgo County
Legal Mechanism: Project finance facility (debt + tax equity)

Action Taken: Issued Public Notice of Meeting at which Commissioners Court would consider approval of a Tax Abatement Agreement for Project Great Kiskadee under Texas Tax Code §312.207; meeting scheduled for June 11, 2024 at 10:00 AM in Edinburg
Legal Mechanism: Texas Tax Code §312.207(c); Hidalgo County Tax Abatement Guidelines (Dec. 13, 2022)

Action Taken: Issued Public Notice of Meeting for Project Great Kiskadee Tax Abatement Agreement; continued/rescheduled consideration
Legal Mechanism: Texas Tax Code §312.207 (notice/hearing)

Entity: Apex Clean Energy / SA Grid Solutions
Action Taken: Construction continued in Hidalgo County under previously-secured permits; project initially anticipated COD Q3 2024 (per Apex financing announcement)
Legal Mechanism: Hidalgo County Planning Department building permit; ERCOT energization process

Entity: Apex Clean Energy / SA Grid Solutions
Action Taken: Announced commercial operations of Great Kiskadee Storage (100 MW / 200 MWh, 2-hour duration); first operational asset of SA Grid Solutions JV; equipment provided by Powin
Legal Mechanism: ERCOT commercial operation declaration

g: REJECTED — Amador Energy Storage (Taaleri / Belltown), Van Zandt County
Action Taken: DISQUALIFIERS: (1) resident lawsuit (Cause No. 24-00204, Dec 2024); (2) Van Zandt County Fire Marshal enforcement order (Mar 2025) under Texas Local Government Code Ch. 233 / NFPA 855; (3) Temporary Restraining Order issued by 294th District Court (Cause No. 25-00067, Apr 2025); (4) county-level green energy moratorium adopted by Van Zandt County Commissioners Court (Feb 11, 2026). See Case 1 above for full timeline.
Legal Mechanism: Multiple disqualifiers

g: REJECTED — Rogers Draw BESS (Peregrine Energy Solutions), Harper, Gillespie County
Action Taken: DISQUALIFIERS: (1) opposing county resolution adopted by Gillespie County Commissioners Court (Feb 24, 2025); (2) resident lawsuit filed in 216th Judicial District Court (Sep 5, 2025); (3) Gillespie County intervention as plaintiff (Nov 26, 2025) and Stop Work Order (Dec 11, 2025); (4) agreed Temporary Injunction signed Jan 14, 2026. See Case 2 above for full timeline.
Legal Mechanism: Multiple disqualifiers

g: REJECTED — Ochoa Energy Storage (Vesper Energy), City of Katy
Action Taken: DISQUALIFIER: SUP/zoning denial — Katy City Council unanimously denied Special Use Permit for the 500 MW Ochoa Energy Storage project on Oct 14, 2024, overriding P&Z Committee recommendation. Ongoing PUC appeal under Docket No. 58859/SOAH 473-26-07508. See Case 3 above for full timeline.
Legal Mechanism: SUP / zoning denial by city council

g: REJECTED — Marshall Springs Solar + BESS (AMPYR Energy USA), Willow City, Gillespie County
Action Taken: DISQUALIFIERS: (1) opposing county resolution adopted by Gillespie County Commissioners Court (Nov 3, 2025); (2) Gillespie County lawsuit filed in District Court (Case No. 26-18599, Mar 11, 2026); (3) county Notice of Applicability of Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance (Dec 1, 2025). See Case 4 above for full timeline.
Legal Mechanism: Multiple disqualifiers

g: REJECTED — OCI Hillsboro Solar / Hillsboro BESS Project, Hill County
Action Taken: INVESTIGATED — Hill County Commissioners Court approved tax abatement with OCI Hillsboro Solar in 2023, and on 2026-01-28 amended the abatement to separate solar and BESS (Hillsboro BESS Project LLC) per Hyundai Engineering's request. Project not selected because (a) BESS portion has not yet reached construction-start or COD (Hyundai Engineering reported delays), and (b) documented citizen protest ("Hill county citizens protest big business projects" Facebook 2026) urging commissioners to vote NO on solar tax abatements. Does not meet "reached construction-start or commercial-operation between 2023 and 2026" criterion for the BESS, and citizen protest activity creates ambiguity vs the no-disqualifier requirement.
Legal Mechanism: Not yet built (BESS) + citizen protest activity

g: REJECTED — Old 300 Storage (Ørsted), Fort Bend County
Action Taken: INVESTIGATED — Co-located 250 MW / 500 MWh BESS adjacent to operating 430 MW Old 300 Solar Center (solar COD Sept 2024). Fort Bend County Commissioners Court considered Tax Abatement on 2024-11-26; Ørsted took Final Investment Decision Q4 2024 and announced new BESS Apr 2025. No disqualifiers identified. NOT SELECTED as a primary case because the BESS itself has not yet reached commercial operations (expected Feb 2026 per Cleanview); construction is ongoing. Could be promoted to a primary case once COD is confirmed.
Legal Mechanism: BESS not yet operational (still under construction as of investigation)

g: --- CASE 2: Gillespie County BESS Blockage — Peregrine Energy Solutions / Rogers Draw BESS, Harper, Gillespie County, TX ---

Action Taken: Passed resolution opposing Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in Gillespie County, citing safety, economic, and environmental concerns. Also passed resolution requiring documentation for Harper BESS project
Legal Mechanism: County resolution (Commissioners Court)

Action Taken: As of this date, Peregrine Energy Solutions had not filed any of the required construction documents with Gillespie County. LCRA connection construction continued.
Legal Mechanism: County documentation requirements (February 24 resolution)

Entity: Victor Hollender, Bruce Neitzke, Esther Schneider, Sarah Rivas (Harper residents)
Action Taken: Filed lawsuit in 216th Judicial District Court against Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC and B&CWR Inc. (d/b/a Cactus Construction), seeking temporary and permanent injunction to stop construction of 145MW BESS due to fire risk and chemical contamination concerns, proximity to Harper ISD (~1,700 feet)
Legal Mechanism: Petition for Temporary and Permanent Injunction — 216th Judicial District Court, Gillespie County

Action Taken: Filed Petition in Intervention in 216th Judicial District Court, joining the residents' lawsuit as a plaintiff opposing the Rogers Draw BESS. Simultaneously filed Response in Opposition to Rogers Draw's Rule 91a Motion to Dismiss
Legal Mechanism: Petition in Intervention — 216th Judicial District Court, Gillespie County (Cause No. 25-18363)

Action Taken: Voted to take action on two energy projects. County announced it would file petition of intervention against Peregrine Energy (confirmed the Nov 26 filing). Residents packed meeting to voice concerns. Evidence of new LCRA utility pole construction found at Harper site
Legal Mechanism: Commissioner's Court meeting

Action Taken: Issued Stop Work Order to Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC for the Rogers Draw BESS project in Harper, Texas
Legal Mechanism: Stop Work Order

Action Taken: Filed countersuit against Gillespie County in 216th Judicial District Court, claiming the county had initially approved the project and reversed course after citizen pressure. Sought dismissal of county's intervention
Legal Mechanism: Counterclaim — 216th Judicial District Court

Entity: 216th District Court Judge Albert Patillo / All parties
Action Taken: Agreed temporary injunction signed halting all construction activities on the Rogers Draw BESS project at Harper until final judgment. Trial date to be set
Legal Mechanism: Agreed Temporary Injunction — 216th Judicial District Court

g: --- CASE 3: Katy BESS Denial — Vesper Energy / Ochoa Energy Storage, Katy, TX (Harris County area) ---

Entity: Vesper Energy / Ochoa Energy Storage LLC
Action Taken: Applied for Special Use Permit (SUP) to build Ochoa Energy Storage (500MW BESS) on approximately 24-acre property at 27051 Highway Blvd, Katy, TX. Holds two ERCOT queue positions (Ochoa I & II) representing 750MW cumulative capacity
Legal Mechanism: Special Use Permit application — City of Katy zoning ordinance

Entity: City of Katy Planning and Zoning Committee
Action Taken: Recommended approval of the Special Use Permit for Ochoa Energy Storage
Legal Mechanism: Planning and Zoning Committee recommendation

Entity: Vesper Energy (Community Affairs Manager Alex Rohr)
Action Taken: Held "lunch and learn" community session for local residents to provide details on Ochoa project and energy storage industry
Legal Mechanism: Community outreach session

Action Taken: Unanimously denied Special Use Permit for Ochoa Energy Storage after public hearing with multiple residents voicing opposition. Overrode Planning and Zoning Committee recommendation. Motion by Councilmember Dan Smith, seconded by Councilmember Janet Corte. Concerns cited: fire safety, proximity to Katy High School and Katy ISD Outdoor Learning Center, residential areas, Falcon Point Apartments
Legal Mechanism: Denial of Special Use Permit — City of Katy zoning authority

Action Taken: Citizens expressed continued opposition at city council meeting. Mayor Pro Tem Chris Harris cited proximity to schools and residential areas
Legal Mechanism: City Council meeting — public comment

Action Taken: Filed appeal and application for declaratory order with Texas Public Utility Commission (PUC Docket No. 58859, SOAH Docket No. 473-26-07508). Argued City of Katy's zoning actions exceed regulatory authority under Public Utility Regulatory Act (PURA). Requested PUC declare city ordinances "null and void." Moved for immediate referral to State Office of Administrative Hearings (SOAH)
Legal Mechanism: Appeal and Application for Declaratory Order — Texas PUC; PURA §39.001(c)(d), §39.151

Action Taken: Filed response to Ochoa's PUC appeal, arguing city ordinances do not interfere with PUC or ERCOT jurisdiction and do not conflict with state authority under PURA
Legal Mechanism: Response to Appeal — Texas PUC Docket No. 58859

Action Taken: Filed with PUC stating direct interest in the outcome of the case, asserting city's denial affects grid authority. PUC decision delayed
Legal Mechanism: Intervention / Statement of Interest — Texas PUC Docket No. 58859

Action Taken: Procedural schedule set: Ochoa direct testimony April 10, 2026; City direct testimony May 15, 2026; Staff and ERCOT testimony June 1, 2026; Cross-rebuttal July 6, 2026; Hearing on the Merits August 4-5, 2026
Legal Mechanism: Procedural Schedule — PUC Docket No. 58859 / SOAH Docket No. 473-26-07508

g: --- CASE 4: Ampyr Energy USA / Marshall Springs Solar and BESS, Willow City, Gillespie County, TX ---

Action Taken: Proposed Marshall Springs Solar Complex and Battery Energy Storage System (~1,100 acres, 150MW solar + 150MW BESS) near Willow City in Gillespie County, TX. AMPYR described as an Indonesian energy investment company with no history of completed projects in Texas or the US
Legal Mechanism: Project proposal / ERCOT interconnection queue

Action Taken: Communicated in writing to AMPYR Energy that the Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance applies to the proposed Marshall Springs project
Legal Mechanism: Written notice — Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance

Action Taken: Passed resolution opposing Solar and BESS projects, specifically targeting the proposed Marshall Springs project. Required Marshall Springs Solar Farm and BESS documentation
Legal Mechanism: County resolution (Commissioners Court)

Action Taken: Sent letter to AMPYR Energy pressing for answers regarding the proposed 1,000-acre BESS and solar facility, citing concerns about Hill Country landscape, drought, wildfire, flash flooding risk, volunteer fire department capacity, and proximity to rural residences
Legal Mechanism: Congressional inquiry letter

Action Taken: Sent formal Notice of Applicability of Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance to the proposed Marshall Springs Solar Complex and Battery Energy Storage System Project
Legal Mechanism: Formal notice — Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance

Entity: Gillespie County Commissioners Court / Gillespie County residents
Action Taken: Residents packed commissioners court meeting to voice continued concerns with both the Marshall Springs and Rogers Draw projects
Legal Mechanism: Commissioner's Court meeting — public comment

Action Taken: Held information meeting with senior representatives of federal, state, county, and city officials. Attendees included aides to U.S. Senator John Cornyn, U.S. Senator Ted Cruz, U.S. Rep. Chip Roy, Texas Senator Pete Flores, and Texas Representative Ellen Troxclair
Legal Mechanism: Citizen-organized informational meeting with elected officials

Action Taken: Signed ERCOT interconnection agreement for 150MW Marshall Springs Solar project in Gillespie County, advancing the development despite local opposition
Legal Mechanism: ERCOT Interconnection Agreement

Action Taken: Sent follow-up letter to AMPYR Energy renewing pressure, requesting answers on Chinese components and funding sources, environmental risks, and community impact
Legal Mechanism: Congressional follow-up inquiry letter

Action Taken: Filed lawsuit (Case No. 26-18599) against Marshall Springs Storage LLC, Marshall Springs Solar LLC, and Ampyr Energy USA LLC in Gillespie County District Court
Legal Mechanism: Lawsuit filing — Gillespie County District Court
